zh.lifeSummary: Ellen Goodwin Sale是William Sale的女兒，於1830年出生在英國斯塔福爾艾可萊沙拉爾。她於1862年2月4日與Thomas Tildesley在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾艾可萊沙拉爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾艾可萊沙拉爾住了大約20年且於1871年住在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾利-法姆斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Ellen Goodwin Sale是William Sale的女儿，于1830年出生在英国斯塔福爾艾可萊沙拉爾。她于1862年2月4日与Thomas Tildesley在英国英格兰斯塔福爾艾可萊沙拉爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她在英国英格兰斯塔福爾艾可萊沙拉爾住了大约20年且于1871年住在英国英格兰斯塔福爾利-法姆斯。

nameMeanings.name: Sale
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Sale (Cheshire), or from any of several minor places or topographic features named from Middle English sale, salle ‘sallow, willow’ (Old English s(e)alh). See also Seal , with which this name may often have interchanged.

English: from Middle English sal(e) ‘hall’ (Old English sæl, Old French sale, Old Norse salr), translated by medieval clerks as Latin aula. The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived or worked in a hall, or occupational, for someone who worked at a hall as a servant. This was the name of an Anglo-Norman family who had settled in County Tipperary, Ireland, by the 13th century. The name was Gaelicized as de Sál, hence the Anglicized form Saul which has been widely adopted in Ireland.
Croatian (Šale): probably from a pet form of the personal name Šanto, a Croatized form of Italian Santo .

variants.name: Sallows
meanings: English (Essex and Suffolk): topographic name from Middle English salwe ‘sallow (a species of willow)’ (Old English s(e)alh), with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. Compare Sale .

variants.name: Sales
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Efren, Alberto, Francisco, Manuel, Camilo, Carlos, Fernando, Juan, Juanita, Mario, Ofelia.

English: variant of Sale with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. The Middle English plural form was probably often in variation with the singular form. The surname is uncommon in southwestern England but may derive there from Zeals (Wiltshire), and also from the plural form of Old English sealh ‘willow’; see Seal .
Catalan, Spanish, and French (Occitan): habitational name from any of the places called Sales, like Sales de Llierca (Catalonia), from the plural of   Sala   1. Compare   De Sales  .

variants.name: Salis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ruben, Amparo, Armando, Emilio, Juana, Rodolfo.
Italian (Sardinia): plural form of   Sale   ‘salt’.
French: variant of Salies, a habitational name from a placename Salies in Haute-Garonne and Pyrénées-Atlantiques, ultimately derived from Latin salina ‘salt marsh, salt pan, saltworks’.

variants.name: Seal
meanings: English: either a habitational name from Seal (Kent), Seale (Surrey), or Sele in Upper Beeding (Sussex), all of which probably derive from Old English sele ‘hall, building’ (though the Surrey placename may arise from Old English sēale, dative form of salh ‘willow’), or else a topographic name for someone who lived at a boggy patch (Kentish Middle English and Old English sele) or a hall. Compare Sale 1-2.
English: habitational name from Overseal or Netherseal (Derbyshire), probably from Old English scegel ‘small wood’.

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a willow copse or at a place marked by a willow or willows, from Middle English sele ‘willow’ (Old English sele), in northern England representing Old Norse selja and in southwestern England representing Old English (West Saxon) sealh. The name was probably interchangeable with the synonymous Middle English sale (Old English salh, Old Norse salr), and in some cases the surname may have functioned as a variant of Sale . In southwestern England initial S- was frequently voiced to Z-, as in the Wiltshire placename Zeals (from the plural form of Old English sealh). However, the medieval form of the placename is overwhelmingly in the plural, and this may also survive as Sales .

variants.name: Saile
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Elke, Kurt, Volker.
English: variant of   Sale  .
German: older form of   Seiler  .

variants.name: Sayle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik.
Manx (Isle of Man): variant of   Sale   1.
Possibly a variant or an Americanized form of German   Saile  .

nameMeanings.name: Ellen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Helen , although now no longer associated with that name. Initial H- tended to be added and dropped rather capriciously, leading to many doublets ( compare for example Esther and Hester ). It is also sometimes used as a short form of Eleanor , to which, however, it is unrelated.

variants.name: Eleanor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

nameMeanings.name: Goodwin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is derived from the Old English personal name Gōdwine, from gōd ‘good’ + wine ‘friend’. There has been considerable confusion with Godwin .

historicEvents.title: The Factory Act Restricts Child Labor
historicEvents.description: The Factory Act restricted the hours women and children could work in textile mills. No child under the age of 9 were allowed to work, and children ages 9-13 could not work longer than 9 hours per day. Children up to the age of 13 were required to receive at least two hours of schooling, six days per week.

historicEvents.title: School Attendance Becomes Mandatory for Children
historicEvents.description: School attendance became compulsory from ages five to ten on August 2, 1880.

historicEvents.title: The Entente Cordiale
historicEvents.description: The Entente Cordiale was signed between Britain and France on April 8, 1904, to reconcile imperial interests and pave the way for future diplomatic cooperation. This ended hundreds of years of conflict between the two states.
London, United Kingdom hosts Summer Olympic Games.

historicEvents.title: British Unemployment Reaches Post-War High
historicEvents.description: British unemployment reached a post-war high in July 1921 of 2.5 million people.
First UK government led by the Labor party under Prime Minister Ramsay MacDonald.

en.lifeSummary: Ellen Goodwin Sale was born in 1830, in Eccleshall, Staffordshire, England as the daughter of William Sale. She married Thomas Tildesley on 4 February 1862, in Eccleshall, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. She lived in Eccleshall, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom for about 20 years and Three-Farms, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom in 1871.

fr.lifeSummary: Ellen Goodwin Sale est née en 1830, à Eccleshall, Staffordshire, Angleterre, fille de William Sale. Elle a épousé Thomas Tildesley le 4 février 1862, à Eccleshall, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Elle a vécu à Eccleshall, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni pendant environ 20 ans et à Three-Farms, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni en 1871.

de.lifeSummary: Ellen Goodwin Sale wurde 1830 in Eccleshall, Staffordshire, England, als Tochter von William Sale geboren. Sie hat am 4. Februar 1862 in Eccleshall, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Thomas Tildesley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. Sie hat ungefähr 20 Jahre lang in Eccleshall, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom gelebt, und 1871 hat sie in Three-Farms, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom gelebt.

it.lifeSummary: Ellen Goodwin Sale è nata il nel 1830 nel Eccleshall, Staffordshire, England come figlia di William Sale. Lei sposato Thomas Tildesley il 4 febbraio 1862, nel Eccleshall, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie. Vissuto a Eccleshall, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito per circa 20 anni e nel Three-Farms, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito nel 1871.

ja.lifeSummary: Ellen Goodwin Saleは1830年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアエククレシャルでWilliam Saleの娘として生まれた。1862年2月4日にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアエククレシャルでThomas Tildesleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアエククレシャルに約20年間，1871年にはイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアスレエ-ファルムズに住んでいた。

ko.lifeSummary: Ellen Goodwin Sale(은)는 1830년 영국 스태퍼드실르 c클샤에서 William Sale의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1862년 2월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 c클샤에서 Thomas Tildesley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 c클샤에서 약 20년, 1871년도에 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 스리-파스에서 살았다.

pt.lifeSummary: Ellen Goodwin Sale nasceu em 1830, em Eccleshall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra filha de William Sale. Ela casou-se com Thomas Tildesley em 4 de fevereiro de 1862, em Eccleshall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. Ela viveu em Eccleshall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom por quase 20 anos e em Three-Farms, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom em 1871.

ru.lifeSummary: Ellen Goodwin Sale родилась в 1830 г. в местности экклешолл, стаффордшир, Англия. Её отца звали William Sale. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Tildesley 4 февраля 1862 г. в местности экклешолл, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Она жила в местности экклешолл, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания около 20 лет и в местности три-фармс, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в 1871 году.

es.lifeSummary: Ellen Goodwin Sale nació en 1830, en Eccleshall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra como hija de William Sale. Se casó con Thomas Tildesley el 4 de febrero de 1862, en Eccleshall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas. Vivió en Eccleshall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido durante unos 20 años y en Three-Farms, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido en 1871.
